prose:
Frontier orbital analysis of water (H₂O) computed with the frontier-orbitals chemkit skill.

Method: DFT, tier 'standard' → B3LYP/def2-TZVP, using PySCF RKS. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). Geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied .xyz file (single-point; not re-optimized at this level). Environment: gas phase (no solvent). Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral, closed-shell singlet).

SCF converged in 7 cycles. No warnings reported (clean run). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true — all integrity checks passed.

Key results:
  HOMO energy:       −8.6037183 eV
  LUMO energy:       +0.33695037 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap:      8.9406687 eV  ← headline value
  Total energy:     −76.462901 Hartree (−2080.6615 eV)

The HOMO–LUMO gap is a Koopmans-theorem orbital energy difference, not an excitation energy or measured optical gap.

CLI invocation: cli.py frontier /home/erobles/.../water.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults
Output JSON: water_frontier_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json
